hypothesis: If open-source generative models are pretrained on large molecular corpora and then fine-tuned on a small CTF-specific dataset and optimized via reinforcement learning with expert-encoded structural rewards, then they will generate a higher proportion of valid, unique, internally diverse, and novel CTF candidates that pass expert filters than the same models trained without one or more of these steps because hierarchical knowledge transfer aligns general chemical priors with task-specific design rules.
